text:
Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou (ハルケンブルグ＝ホイコーロ, Harukenburugu Hoikōro) is the Ninth Prince of the Kakin Empire's fourteen Princes, fourth and youngest of Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou and his second wife, Second Queen Duazul Hui Guo Rou's two daughters and two sons. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 Halkenburg was born to Nasubi's first wife, First Queen Unma Hui Guo Rou, but was adopted by Duazul. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 403 He originally had two older brothers, the older is 1 st Prince Benjamin Hui Guo Rou and the younger is 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou, but after his adoption, his new set of siblings comprises two older adoptive full-sisters and one older adoptive full-brother; his oldest sister is 2 nd Prince Camilla Hui Guo Rou, his younger older sister is 5 th Prince Tubeppa Hui Guo Rou, and his older brother is 7 th Prince Luzurus Hui Guo Rou. It is rumored on international websites that the reason for Halkenburg's adoption was to protect him from assassination plots from his older siblings. 

Appearance

Halkenburg is a tall and slim young man with has blond, shaggy hair, and a rectangular face. He has relatively small black eyes with thin eyebrows tapering upwards, and a wide, flat nose.

Fitting with his scholarly background and his previously-expressed views of wanting to abolish the monarchy, he is normally dressed professionally. Halkenburg is never seen dressed in anything more casual than a suit, with the exceptions of when he first woke up after collapsing on the first day of the voyage, and when he was sick from an infection. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 404 

Personality

Halkenburg's personality undergoes change and growth throughout the story. 

He is described to have the best qualities among the Princes, but has made public his "misfortune" of never getting along with his mother Duazul or his two sisters, and that (his brother) Tserriednich is the one Prince he accepts. He openly criticizes the royals, and Kurapika speculates that he feels discontent and alienation. Oito confirms that he is very strict with himself and others, and shares that he intends to change the politics of the royal family. He demonstrates foresight several times, even before making an appearance in the story, by being considerate enough of his popularity and that others would target him with assassins to not make a listing seeking bodyguards. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 

He is also a pacifist, convicted enough to communicate to his father his refusal to win through fratricide and sororicide. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 361 He leads his men in a moment of silence after the demise of his sister Prince Momoze, saddened enough by it to tear up. His determination to prevent his siblings from dying outweighs the risk of imprisonment, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 willing to threaten friendly soldiers at gunpoint to further this goal. He has a good relationship with his subordinates as well, shocked at finding his men all inexplicably unconscious. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 When he wakes up after collapsing, his men are friendly and display concern for him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

Although it took a visible emotional toll on him, Halkenburg was willing to kill his father and start a revolution to stop the succession contest and change Kakin. When he failed, he immediately attempted suicide to escape Nasubi's machinations. However, he survived and was swayed by his father's philosophy, who told him that the lives of the princes mean little against those of the people and that whatever change Halkenburg wants to make, he must make himself after seizing power. This made him willing to win the right to the crown. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 On the day of the banquet, Prince Halkenburg is briefly immobilized by the music of Melody's flute performance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 383 

Background

Halkenburg was born to Nasubi's first wife, Unma, who had already given Nasubi several children before Halkenburg. For an unknown reason, Nasubi's second wife, Duazul, began to raise him; it is rumored on in-universe international websites that Unma had asked this as a secret favor of Duazul, in order to protect Halkenburg from assassination plots from his older siblings by changing his order in line to the throne, though it is unclear how exactly order to inherit the Kakin throne is determined.

While in the grade school, he was admitted into a boarding one. The isolation started the rumors of him being exiled or was hiding from assassins. He posted on Facebox that Tserriednich is the only sibling that accepts him. At 15, he skipped grade to attend the world's most prestigious university, Miwal. He studied physics there and also won a silver medal in the world championships of archery he participated in. At some point, he proposed a change in the Kakin royal family's government which garnered him supporters, but also enemies. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

A Black Sheep among the Princes

Halkenburg is first introduced reading in front of a well-furnished bookcase as the son of 2 nd Queen Duazul. In the hopes of recovering the Kurta Clan's Scarlet Eyes from Halkenburg's half-brother Tserriednich, and with the knowledge of Halkenburg public declaration of his acceptance of Tserriednich, Kurapika tries to apply for an anonymous listing he hopes was made by Halkenburg. His potential employer turns out to be 8 th Queen Oito, who made the listing for her infant daughter 14 th Prince Woble in the hopes of finding someone smart enough to try and seek Halkenburg as an employer despite the limited information available. Oito reveals that Halkenburg did not make a listing because he expected to be flooded with responses and to avoid assassins infiltrating his guard. 

Oito describes Halkenburg as being strict with himself and others, and shares that Halkenburg wants to change the royal family politics, with followers both within and without the slowly-strengthening faction. She also mentions the never-ending rumors of assassination plots, and that there are followers that scheme to use him to form a dictatorship. As part of her revelation to Kurapika of the succession contest's true nature as a fourteen-way battle to the death, Oito says that those in the weakest positions like her plan to use Halkenburg's participation in the battle as blackmail to buy their safety. 

On August 8 th , the eve of the Kakin Empire's voyage to the Dark Continent, Halkenburg is seen with the rest of the Princes and Beyond Netero on a stage, smiling and waving at the crowd while dressed in a suit. Kakin's Royal Family is flown into the Black Whale 1 shortly after. Halkenburg is next seen speaking with guests together with Tserriednich during a ceremony taking place in the Black Whale 1. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Halkenburg is next seen the next day, the first day of the voyage, during the departure ceremony in the banquet hall of Tier 1. One by one, the Princes are notified by Nugui of their time to retire. Halkenburg is speaking with guests when called to retire, but when he is, tells Nugui of his intent to speak with his father instead of departing. Halkenrburg approaches his father, who dismisses his guests to speak with Halkenburg privately. 

Halkenburg tells his father that he wishes to withdraw from the succession battle, explaining that he participated in the ceremony to save his father face and that he does not desire a bloody throne. As Halkenburg speaks, Nasubi observes Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast perched idly on his shoulders, a feathered, ogre-like cyclops-gargoyle, almost too big to rest on Halkenburg's shoulders. Nasubi chuckles and accepts his request. 

Halkenburg returns to Room 1009, his quarters, where he is horrified to find his men unconscious and collapsed on the floor of his living room. The sound of a feather wooshing through the air is heard, and Halkenburg is next seen four hours after departure, waking up in his bed wearing sleepwear instead of his suit. Halkenburg immediately rushes out of his master bedroom to check on the situation in his living room, but sees his guards all awake and unpacking his possessions. His men ask how he feels, but he does not respond, still confused. Halkenburg wonders if what he saw was a dream, but sees the mark of a pinion on the back of his left hand, confirming that something did happen. 

Later that day, Halkenburg is contacted by Room 1014 and agrees to send his bodyguards to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 At 9:00 a.m. the next day, Yuhirai and Shedule turn up at Room 1014 to represent him. Shedule has a brief flashback to the previous day, where Halkenburg and his men had discovered that they all had the mark of a pinion on the backs of their left hands. Halkenburg tasked them with finding out what the mark meant, speculating that it had to do with the Nen beasts that a guard in Room 1014 had called about, and giving the two permission to divulge any information they have to offer in order to learn all they could. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

In the morning of the third day, Halkenburg gathers his bodyguards, and leads them in a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze, who had died on the night of the first day. Unbeknownst to Halkenburg, the unison of action causes an enormous amount of aura to be produced. This is noted by Shikaku, a bodyguard sent by 1 st Prince Benjamin in the afternoon of the first day to guard and spy on Halkenburg. Shikaku notes that each individual's aura was average, but rose suddenly in power once assembled with Halkenburg, a symbiotic fellowship that could become a threat to Benjamin. The amount of aura frightens Shikaku enough to immediately contact Benjamin for instructions. 

While waiting for a response, Shikaku thinks of how to kill Halkenburg, and worries about possible retaliation from his Guardian Spirit Beast, noting its knowledge of his ability to use Nen and its constant alertness. He remains wary of being shot by one of its feathers, believing it has the manipulator ability to induce memory revision. He also considers the possibility that the beast's ability is counteractive, which would improve its ability to delete and tamper with memories, and worries about it changing his target from Halkenburg to Benjamin. Right as Shikaku decides to hold off attacking, Benjamin contacts him to ask if the aura he feels in Room 1001 is Halkenburg. Shikaku confirms this, that he cannot kill Halkenburg, and that he will need two people to "verify information", promising to explain more later. 

After the moment of silence, Halkenburg begins walking towards the main door of his room. The Captain of his Guards asks him where he is going, and after hearing Halkenburg say that he is going to make another appeal to his father, reminds him that seeing the King outside the banquets is forbidden. Halkenburg accepts the risk of disqualification and insists on going, and his captain tries to stop him again, reminding him that Nasubi had declared that the battle for succession could not be cancelled, and that further resistance could get him arrested. Halkenburg turns and faces him, and with tears in his eyes, states that if another victim happens and he does nothing, he might as well have killed them himself. His guards are unable to further stop him, and instead, five of them accompany him. Halkenburg instructs the rest to continue their duties before leaving. Shikaku reports his movement to Benjamin. 

At the VVIP gate, Halkenburg's five men stand guard as he speaks with the guard at the gate. Shikaku reports to his superiors that Halkenburg is negotiating with the guard. Halkenburg is refused entry and instead asks for a letter to be given to Nasubi, but the interlocutor tells him the best that he can do is hand it to the Grand Chamberlain, with advice to give it to Nasubi directly at the next banquet. Halkenburg insists on leaving the letter there, and departs with a promise to return daily until Nasubi sees him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

A New Ability and a New Resolve

During Halkenburg's fourth consecutive day of visits, the guard at the VVIP gate greet him, and with the familiarity of his routine the past three days, preemptively but respectfully asks for his letter. Halkenburg faintly smiles, then reaches into his inner pocket and pulls out a gun. With finger on the trigger, he tells the guard at the VVIP gate not to move. His men do the same with the three royal army soldiers present, who had not stayed on alert. With relief that he made it in time before another Prince was sacrificed, Halkenburg walks through the areas behind the VVIP gate with gun drawn and approaches Nasubi, watching scenes of nature on a large television with his back toward the entrance of his room on a swivel chair. 

Halkenburg walks wordlessly towards his father with gun raised, hoping to have his father agree to put an end to the succession contest. Without turning, Nasubi tells him that he is too late. Halkenburg says that there is still time, and Nasubi turns his head and tells him that he consented to the ritual. In anger, Halkenburg says that he was never told that he and his siblings were to kill each other. Nasubi calmly tells Halkenburg that it is an important part of the process to be able to adapt to new situations, and Halkenburg threatens to shoot if Nasubi does not suspend the succession battle. Nasubi swivels to face Halkenburg, and tells him that the battle for succession is out of his hands, and he could not do anything about it anymore. He refuses to call it off, saying that the battle is for Halkenburg and his siblings. He chastises Halkenburg for his hypocrisy, telling him to take action instead of letting others do the work for him, and that there is nothing more disgusting than hypocrisy. 

Halkenburg does not respond, and after a moment, steadies the gun with his other hand. As he aims the gun at his father, he declares that with a revolution, he will become King and put a stop to the battle, then wishes his father goodbye and shoots. The bullet crumples mid-air mere centimeters away from Nasubi's forehead, and Halkenburg freezes in shock. Nasubi reaches out and takes the crumpled bullet, and compliments Halkenburg on his aim before reminding him to always fire three shots when shooting to kill. Halkenburg continues watching in shock, and Nasubi discards the bullet as he tells Halkenburg that he is part of the ritual now, and cannot die until it ends. Nasubi calls over Nugui, who had just appeared at the entrance with three soldiers. Nugui tells Halkenburg that the situation was accounted for before the ship's departure as a secret training exercise, and that Halkenburg and his guards will not be punished. 

Wanting to take charge of the situation, Halkenburg aims the pistol at his head and pulls the trigger, but once again, the bullet is stopped. Though Halkenburg is unable to see it, Nasubi notes that Halkenburg's Nen beast agrees with him and had stopped the bullet with its finger-like claws, and asks Halkenburg if he is awake now. Nasubi asks Halkenburg if he had heard of the trolley problem, and Halkenburg asks if Nasubi was saying that the countrymen were on one track, and his siblings on the other. Nasubi laughs at his response, telling him that him thinking it was a binary choice is unlike him and a sign that he is not thinking straight. Nasubi tells him that what must survive is the country, and that the people's lives always come first, the only question being who pulls the lever. Nasubi solemnly tells Halkenburg that he cannot be king without bearing the cross, and cannot change the country without being king. As Halkenburg leaves, Nasubi tells him to become a king before declaring that he did not need one. 

Back in Room 1009, Shikaku is roused from his sleep by Benjamin through a earpiece. He confirms that it is unnatural for him to be sleeping while Halkenburg is out, and suggests that his Nen beast had attacked him. Benjamin lets him know of Halkenburg's revolution as Shikaku rushes out of his bed to check on Room 1009. In Room 1009's master bedroom, Shikaku reports that Halkenburg and his men are all gone, and prepares to head over. Benjamin tells him not to worry since the King is safe, and reveals that Halkenburg had gone to kill Nasubi, though he failed and the situation cooled off and was treated as a drill. As Halkenburg steps through Room 1009's entrance, Benjamin warns Shikaku that Halkenburg had steeled himself, awakened and ready to go all-out. He gives Shikaku the order to prioritize Halkenburg's elimination, and instructs Shikaku to procure Halkenburg's ability. 

As aura swells around Halkenburg and his men, Halkenburg begins to draw a Nen bow. Shikaku communicates that he is in battle, and activates his Culdecept to capture Halkenburg's ability. Protected by the aura of his men, Halkenburg finishes drawing his bow, aiming it directly at Shikaku. With a new appreciation of wielding the forces of war and combat, Halkenburg unleashes his bow, piercing Shikaku's ability and transferring the consciousness of his man Sumidori into Shikaku. Both Shikaku and Sumidori collapse, after a moment, Shikaku rises and salutes Halkenburg, now possessed by Sumidori. From Room 1014, Kurapika notes the second time he felt the powerful aura, and reminds himself to keep track of its occurences. 

During the Sunday banquet on the eighth day of the voyage, Halkenburg is briefly transfixed by the music of Melody's flute performance. 

Jumping back a few days, This scene was surrounded by scenes that took place on the ninth day of the voyage, however, information from Chapter 404 makes this taking place then illogical. The exact date the scene took place in is unclear, but is most likely on the sixth or seventh day. Halkenburg and his men, including a Sumidori-possessed Shikaku, stand around Sumidori's body, hooked up to a monitor. They note that Sumidori appears to simply be asleep, and speculate what happened to Shikaku's soul. Halkenburg decides that they need to find out, and after restating that one of their soul possesses and takes over a target A, suggests four possibilities for what happens to A's soul and where it is. Though aware that there is no way to currently prove or disprove any of them, Halkenburg summons his men and shares his hypothesis with them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the ninth day, Halkenburg's men gather once again for another discussion about his ability. Halkenburg caveats his theory as being based on a gut feeling and states that he needs a Nen expert to back him up if possible, but asks Sumidori if he is willing to do something that Halkenburg believes is absolutely necessary to understand the ability. With a smile, Sumidori agrees to the task, and Halkenburg thanks him with a sad look in his eyes. 

In the neighboring Room 1007, 7 th Prince Luzurus' guards feel the rumbling of aura and confirm that it is from Room 1009. The doorbell rings, and one of Luzurus' guards reports that Shikaku is standing outside the door. Through the intercom system, they ask him what he wants, but instead of responding to their question, Shikaku proclaims Benjamin before shooting himself in the head. Rooms 1001 and 1007 scramble to make sense of the situation. In Room 1009, Sumidori's body, with hands bound behind his back, slowly regains consciousness. He sees himself facing Halkenburg and addresses him by name. Halkenburg asks him to state his post and service number. 

From Room 1014 at 11:30 a.m. on the tenth day, Kurapika feels Halkenburg's ability for a fourth time. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 After hearing a report from Kanjidol in Room 1007 the previous day about their observations of Shikaku's suicide, Benjamin and the Captain of his Guards, Balsamilco, also feel the rumbling, and immediately contact Vict to learn what is happening. They hear Vict signal an emergency to them, and hear a garbled message from Vict about Halkenburg, a bow, him being invincible, and attacks not doing something. They debate on how to deal with Halkenburg, and Balsamilco contacts the justice department to have him arrested under suspicion of premeditated murder, with plans to also redeploy his men to the lower tiers in order to separate Halkenburg from them. Men from the Justice Bureau come to effect the arrest, despite the protests of Halkenburg's men. After the arrest, Balsamilco tells Benjamin that they would get him at their opening trial. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

The Death of a Prince

While alone in an unknown location at 1:30 p.m. on the tenth day, Balsamilco tests out an emission device hidden in his shoe, and loads a vial of TSK-17 into it after confirming that it is functioning as expected. Balsamilco holds the trigger in his hand and vows to delete Halkenburg with it. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 402 

In Kaiser's office on Tier 2 at 8:50 a.m. of the eleventh day, Melody brings up a rumbling that happened right above where they were in the Ministry of Justice and guesses that it was related to the ability of Halkenburg or his Guardian Spirit Beast. Melody notes that Halkenburg had fallen ill, and stresses the importance of knowing whether it was intentional or unexpected. Melody tells Kaiser that she would like to know all the details about it, and to give him a letter containing information that the Ministry of Justice had gathered on Benjamin's secret, Tserriednich's depravity, and Halkenburg's own birth to him, expecting him to make use of it. 

At 6:45 a.m. on the eleventh day, Balsamilco arrives at the Tier 2 Ministry of Justice prosecution and courthouse area entrance for the trial between Benjamin and Halkenburg. As he gets checked, he mentally reviews the locations of Halkenburg's former security detail and concludes that since they were separated from him, Halkenburg had no one around him to produce the powerful rumbling. He moves on to thinking about his plan to kill Halkenburg with TSK-17, planning to infect him with it as he approaches Halkenburg during cross examination, which would have Halkenburg isolated enough that no one else was likely to be infected. As Balsamilco walks down a corridor, he pauses. He slowly turns and sees Vict staring at him from behind. Balsamilco begins to feel a rumbling, and is shocked and confused, unable to understand what the source of the rumbling was, since Halkenburg had none of his guards with him. Balsamilco ignores the civilians around him and turns to Vict to ask him what had happened. Vict begins to gesture in military sign language, communicating to Balsamilco that he had figured out the secret to Halkenburg's ability. The civilians Balsamilco ignore slowly turn to look at him and whisper his position to a man nearby on a laptop, who uses his monitor to hide his hands and secretly gesture Balsamilco's position to Halkenburg, standing a distance around the corner with his ability powered by civilian supporters and arrow drawn. Halkenburg makes some mental calculations, adjusts his aim, and releases The Boy Who Shoots the Arrow: Grimmel the Dissonance. 

In his room in the VVIP area, Benjamin receives a call, informing him that the trial between Halkenburg and Balsamilco had not begun since both sides had requested a change to the start time. He hears that Balsamilco was unprepared due to equipment issues, and the judge had denied this request, but the judge had accepted Halkenburg himself feeling ill as a reason to change the start time, and the trial had been postponed until Halkenburg feels better. Benjamin wonders if "equipment" issues mean that the mission was not executed, but reasons that because Halkenburg's ability had gone off first, its target could only logically be Balsamilco. He wonders why Halkenburg had fallen ill if Balsamilco was the one attacked, and briefly considers that it could be the result of TSK-17 before scrapping that idea as it was too early for its symptoms to appear. Benjamin asks for more information on Halkenburg's condition, and learns that he suddenly felt sick and collapsed on the spot but was now resting in the sick bay, and that ten or so of his supporters were with him when he collapsed. Benjamin deduces that Halkenburg's ability can be powered by any supporter with sufficient loyalty, regardless of what organization they belong to. 

Benjamin is notified that Balsamilco wants to speak with him over the phone and is immediately wary of a trap. He guesses that Halkenburg was faking illness and Balsamilco would not be talking of his own volition, which would imply that Halkenburg's ability is a coercive manipulation ability, and that despite what Balsamilco had said about "first come first serve" when manipulation abilities were concerned, it would not apply against a powerful Nen formed by a unified group working collectively. 

Balsamilco comes to the phone and explains that he had not executed his mission because of trouble on Halkenburg's side, which postponed the trial. He asks Benjamin if he had heard the rumbling, and after hearing Benjamin say that he did, Balsamilco explains that a Nen arrow was fired at him but missed and Halkenburg lost consciousness on the spot, suggesting that it was because of overuse or because civilians powered his ability this time instead of his personal guards as usual. Benjamin accepts this explanation as plausible, but keeps in mind his belief that Halkenburg's illness was faked, and prepares to institute martial law and take action against Balsamilco if he attempts to return without completing his mission. Balsamilco points out that the trial being postponed was a chance for them, and asks for permission to see Halkenburg. Still wary of a trap, Benjamin tells Balsamilco to do as he wishes as long as results were produced. Balsamilco acknowledges this and takes the chance to say that he was aware of the suspicious position he was in, and tells Benjamin that he would not return until his mission was completed, and promises to get results within the day. 

Shortly after, 1 st Queen Unma receives a call from Halkenburg, notifying her of his plans and telling her that he would not be attending the banquet on Day 15. Unma supports him, and lets her son Halkenburg know that she was proud of him. At roughly the same time, Benjamin reads a letter containing 10 th Prince Kacho's last message to him, which reveals her knowledge that Unma is Halkenburg's real mother, and that the prevailing theory on international websites is that Unma had asked a secret favor of Duazul to protect Halkenburg from assassination plots from his older siblings by changing his order in line to the throne. Kacho writes that the order has little meaning in the succession battle, but if the rumors were true, Unma might be using her power to pull the strongs for Halkenburg. Kacho implies the existence of other information she can leak and asks Benjamin to protect her sister 11 th Prince Fugetsu. After the letters are delivered, Melody, "Kacho", and Kaiser discuss their next steps, and bring up the fact that Halkenburg had been unable to receive his letter because he was under medical treatment before discussing their next steps. 

Later that day, while walking in the Justice Department, Kaiser and Steiner talk about the recent events surrounding Halkenburg. Steiner brings up the mass syncope episodes, but Kaiser says that it is alright because Halkenburg had given them notification about it. He describes Halkenburg telling him to not be alarmed if people experience effects similar to mass hypnosis after meeting him, attributing it to a combination of the joy of basking in his presence and the suddenly release from anticipation, and that they would recover quickly. Steiner brings up a point that concerns him, that one staff member who was with Halkenburg during his visitation period after he had collapsed had himself passed out with the same symptoms, but at a different time and place from the rest. Kaiser is suspicious and asks for the name of location of the staff member to try and find out what Halkenburg is trying to do and form an alliance with him, though he remains wary of how Halkenburg might interpret his actions considering Halkenburg's moral considerations, and decides to avoid meeting Halkenburg for the moment while his ability remains unknown. 

Kaiser pays a visit to Worio Bay, the staff member, and tells him of his knowledge of Nen abilities surrounding the succession battle, and that as an individual, he supports Halkenburg as long as Fugetsu's safety could be assured. He sees Worio show him the back of his hand, and explains that he had received the mark there from the Prince. Worio tells Kaiser that Halkenburg had instructed him to tell him everything if Kaiser had brought up the topic of Nen abilities himself, and had anticipated that Kaiser would come to see him, aware that Kaiser would connect the dots from the mass fainting and telling Worio to interpret Kaiser visiting him instead of Halkenburg as a sign that Kaiser was wary of him and knows of the existence of Nen. Worio tells Kaiser that Halkenburg is going to die soon. 

In Benjamin's residence in the VVIP area, he receives a call notifying him that the mission had been completed. "Balsamilco" tells him that as planned, results would be delivered that night. Unbeknownst to Benjamin, Halkenburg possesses Balsamilco and controls him. 

Kaiser turns up at the sick bay to visit Halkenburg. Halkenburg's body sleeps while surrounded by his supporters. Kaiser stands a distance away from the group. 

Halkenburg's condition deteriorates and his body is rushed to the general hospital on Tier 3, where Cheadle leads a team comprising of Leorio and three other doctors to take charge of Halkenburg's condition. One doctor notifies her that Halkenburg is unresponsive, and another tells her that his temperature is 97.9, without tremors or labored breathing. Cheadle suggests that Halkenburg might have hit his head when he fell, and tells them to order a CHEM-7 blood panel and a CT scan. She also instructs them to ask his guards on Tier 1 about his condition over the past week, and to have his vomitus sent to Gel in forensics. Halkenburg lies in the fetal position on a stretcher as he is rushed through the Hospital. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 404 

"Balsamilco" observes this and notes that Balsamilco's persona (in Halkenburg's body) would not wake up until he (in Balsamilco's body) goes to sleep, and that the addition of a coma to the symptoms would conveniently delay the diagnosis of stomach flu. By getting his mother to refuse an autopsy, he expects his death to be officially classified as unexplained organ failure. "Balsamilco" contacts Benjamin to notify him that Halkenburg had vomited and was taken to the hospital, and that his mission would be complete in about twelve hours. Benjamin notes that it would take roughly twelve hours until death and tells "Balsamilco" that he would have the royal and military medical teams sent to prevent any interference, and to continue monitoring progress. 

Halkenburg thinks about his ability. Because Sumidori's body awoke and recited Shikaku's service number after Sumidori's mind controlled Shikaku's body to commit suicide, Halkenburg concluded that his ability, a forced mind swap between a random person in his group and the target with only one person awake at a time, allowed for his side to have priority of consciousness, a priority earned by putting their lives on the line. Shikaku's mind in Sumidori's body would not awaken until Sumidori's mind in Shikaku's body was no longer awake. Halkenburg next wished to find out what happens to the minds of those involved when their bodies died. His conclusion was that only one person can be awake at a time, and because of the ambiguous definition of 'death' in the succession battle, it was reasonable to assume that both body and mind need to be dead before the battle considers a Prince dead. Because of this requirement, Halkenburg believes that there was no need to test a process which allows the target's mind to return to their original body. He decided to next test 

What happens when to the mind within the target's body when it died? 
What happens if the target's mind, having lost a body to return to, loses its current body? 

Halkenburg answers the latter with his understanding of his ability being made possible with the assistance of the Guardian Spirit Beast in order to win the succession battle, and the former with his belief that only those who are willing to sacrifice themselves for the greater good can participate in his ability; feeling morally conflicted about it is a disservice to their righteous dedication to Halkenburg's cause. He remembers to remain his focus on fighting to build the ideal nation and honor their sacrifices. 

Halkenburg thinks about his current situation, how the probability of him being the one chosen to swap with Balsamilco was low but considered, and prepared for by experimenting with Vict in advance. If the body on Halkenburg's side dies first, the victim's mind returns to their body and coexists with the trespasser's mind, but gets dominance for consciousness. In his situation, after his body dies, Balsamilco's mind will return to his own body and force his mind into sleep. To counter this, Halkenburg plans to make Balsamilco's body take a sleeping pill right before his own body dies, allowing him roughly ten hours to finish his next move to be the victor in the succession battle. 

The royal and military medical teams arrive and force Cheadle and her team to defer responsibility over Halkenburg to them. In the early hours of the next morning, Halkenburg's body dies. "Balsamilco" contacts Benjamin and tells him that Halkenburg's body would be transferred at noon that day, from Tier 3 to Tier 2 via the central staircase, and then the morgue in Tier 1. He also confirms for Benjamin that Halkenburg's final wishes were a ship-wide announcement, a send-off by his supporters, and his personal guards to transport the casket, before receiving instructions from Benjamin to do as Halkenburg wished. Benjamin allows this to be done to show that he honored Halkenburg's wishes, and tells Balsamilco to have everything done solemnly and with dignity to prevent his supporters' sorrow from turning into anger. Benjamin also instructs "Balsamilco" to inform "Clea" that they would drop all charges so that Halkenburg's guards could join him at once. "Balsamilco" acknowledges this, and tells Benjamin that he would discuss funeral plans and the autopsy and monitor the process, meaning that he would return at the same time as Halkenburg's body. Benjamin reminds "Balsamilco" to have the matter handled respectfully, and tells him that his job was well-done. Halkenburg, in Balsamilco's body, thanks his brother in turn. 

On the twelfth day of the voyage, an announcement is made on Tier 3, informing listeners that Halkenburg had passed in the early hours that morning and that the funeral procession will pass through the central staircase on its way to Tier 1 at noon. Those on Tier 3 who wish to pay their respects are asked to gather in the central staircase square. Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan listen to the announcement, and confirm that Halkenburg was not a benefactor for a mafia family before resuming their plans to head back down to Tier 4. 

Later, Morena speaks to some of her underlings and notifies them that Halkenburg's funeral procession is taking place, and that it was a perfect opportunity to act because a lot of people would gather for his send-off. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 405 

Soldiers come across Lynch's corpse somewhere on Tier 3 during a final sweep ahead of the procession. After Hinrigh is teleported back from the Heil-Ly secret base, he reunites with Zakuro and comments on the crowd gathering to send off Halkenburg, remarking that Halkenburg was popular with the people. A Xi-Yu Family underling pushes the crowd aside to get to Hinrigh and notify him of what happened to Lynch. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 406 

Outside the general hospital at around noon, soldiers guard barricade lines as a large crowd gathers to send Halkenburg off. An announcer lets the crowd know that the funeral procession will be passing through shortly, and that a massive crowd is expected. The announcer asks the crowd to remember that the procession is a respectful event, and reminds them not to run or push others and to observe in silence, before wishing that Halkenburg rests in peace. 

Spectators hold back tears and look sorrowfully upon the procession as Halkenburg's casket is carried from the general hospital. Soldiers escort twelve of Halkenburg's guards, acting as pallbearers for his casket, as they walk along a long carpet through the middle of the gathered crowd. The casket is carried up the staircase from Tier 3 to Tier 2. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 407 

In the afternoon of the twelfth day of the voyage, special martial law is declared. At the Justice Bureau, Mizaistom asks Benjamin to provide his reason for declaring special martial law. As part of his explanation, Benjamin claims that Halkenburg had collaborated with Tserriednich to orchestrate terrorism with a biological weapon brought on board by a collaborator in his military. Benjamin mentions that several co-conspirators were accompanying the Princes as they were escorted, and Kaiser wonders if Benjamin was pushing as hard as he was because he had been infected. Using Halkenburg's progression as a point of reference, Kaiser guesses that Benjamin might have die in less than 24 hours. Hunter × Hunter - Chapter 410 

Halkenburg's Employees

Abilities & Powers

As a prince of Kakin, Halkenburg holds significant political power and wealth, although not on the level of the elder Princes. The fact he was able to attend the most prestigious university in the world at the age of 15 speaks for an extreme degree of academic intelligence. Furthermore, his intellect is brilliant, causing Kurapika to state that even among his siblings, Halkenburg's achievements excel and stand out. His education should have provided him with impeccable knowledge of physics. Halkenburg is presumably one of the best archers in the world, having won a silver medal in the world championships of archery he participated in. He has a natural charisma since he has a lot of supporters. He has shown to possess a talent for strategy when he used his letters to cause the royal army to lower their guard. He has also given proof of notable analytical abilities while speculating on how his newfound power works without having any prior knowledge of Nen. 

Nen

After making his resolve to win the succession contest, Halkenburg developed a Nen ability. Balsamilco concluded that it was bestowed on him by his Guardian Spirit Beast. 

Guardian Spirit Beast

Like the rest of his siblings, Halkenburg received an egg from which a Guardian Spirit Beast, a variety of Nen beast, hatched to protect him. As a parasitic-type ability, he has no control over it. The Guardian Spirit Beast is subjected to two rule-like instincts: it is unable to fight other Guardian Spirit Beasts as well as to directly attack the host of one.

Trivia

The ability that Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast endowed him with has similarities to Golem's ability. Just like Halkenburg, Golem forms the 'gun', and a ride-along Emitter provides the bullets. Both characters and abilities were introduced in the Succession Contest arc.
From how he draws his bow, it is likely that Halkenburg is left-handed. Halkenburg also waves to the crowd during the eve festival with his left hand. However, when drawing his gun on the guard at the VVIP gate on the sixth day, Halkenburg holds the gun with his right hand.

Intertextuality and References

Halkenburg shooting himself only for his Guardian Spirit Beast to catch the bullet may be a reference to a scene in the first chapter of JoJo's Bizarre Adventures Stardust Crusaders arc, where Jotaro demonstrates his Stand via the same method.
The name of Halkenburg's ability comprises three references:
"The Boy Who Shoots the Arrow" (, shounen wa zankokuna yumi wo iru; lit. "the boy shoots a cruel bow") is the Japanese title for the 2011 film We Need to Talk About Kevin, which depicts a mother struggling to come to terms with her psychopathic son and his murder spree committed with a bow and arrow.
"Grimmel" (グリマル, lit. "Grimal") refers to David Grimal, a classically trained French violinist.
"The Dissonance" (レ・ディソナンス, lit. "Les Dissonances") refers to Les Dissonances, a classical music orchestra created by David Grimal. It is self conducted and varies in size according to the requirements of the repertoire, having absolute freedom in terms of interpretation.
Perhaps coincidentally, the frequently-referenced J-Pop idol group Keyakizaka46 has a song titled (Fukyouwaon, lit. Dissonance).
The song's lyrics include the line (boku o massatsu shite kara ike, lit. eliminate me first). This 抹殺 is the same term Balsamilco uses in his monologue when he describes deleting Halkenburg in Chapter 402, but with the explicit reading of デリート (deriito, lit. delete) instead of one of its normal Japanese readings, written as .
In the opening scene of the song's music video, one performers lies on the ground in the same position that Halkenburg found several of his men in when he first returned to his room after the departure ceremony in Chapter 362.
In one scene of the song's music video, the performers draw bows in the same manner that Halkenburg does in Chapter 382 when he first uses his ability.
In one scene of the song's music video, the performers draw bows in unison, in the same manner that Halkenburg does in Chapter 403 when he aims his bow at Balsamilco, though Halkenburg draws it with his left hand instead of his right.

Miscellaneous

In the Viz translation of Chapter 404, Cheadle identifies Halkenburg as "Seventh Prince Halkenberg", in a likely mistake made during translation.
